license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.2aegabe
title: gmcSCRB-seq protocol
author: Johannes JWB Bagnoli

steps_raw.instruction: Prepare 96 well plate(s) containing 5 μL Lysis Buffer per well. Lysis plates should be prepared shortly before use, but can be stored for up to 1 week at room temperature. Prior to use, double check to make sure salts have not fallen out of solution or that the lysis buffer has not evaporated. undetermined, Lysis Buffer

Prepare Lysis Buffer for the number of plates needed. Note that 96-well plates are the preferred setup for gmcSCRB-seq, as the well cleanup (Step 9-15) would be more difficult with 384-well plates. %s %s
Sort 1 cell to each well of a 96 well plate containing 5 μL Lysis Buffer.
Immediately seal the plate with an aluminium cold storage seal.
In a cooled centrifuge, spin down the plate for 30 sec @ max. speed and place immediately on dry ice. Undetermined, Store plates containing single-cell lysates in a -80 °C freezer for up to 6 months.
Thaw plates briefly (up to 1 min) at room temperature
Spin down (30 sec @ 1000 rcf) in a centrifuge pre-cooled to 4 °C. Undetermined
Add 10 uL of Clean-up Beads to each well (1:2 ratio). 10 µL, Clean-up Beads

Prepare Reverse Transcription Mix as follows: %s %s - RT MM can be prepared while plate is incubating or while drying on magnet. - If ERCCs will be used, decrease the amount of H2O and add appropriate amount of ERCCs. - Caution: Reverse Transcription Mix with PEG needs to be mixed carefully! Undetermined, Keep Reverse Transcription Mix on ice
Add 5 μL Reverse Transcription Mix to each well. undetermined, Reverse Transcription Mix If a robot (eg. Formulatrix Mantis) is used, make sure to calibrate correctly to the viscous solution.
Seal plate with a PCR seal, vortex briefly and spin down (30 sec @ 1000 rcf) in a centrifuge pre-cooled to 4 °C.
In a thermocycler with heated lid, incubate: Undetermined, 90 min Undetermined, ∞
Pool all wells (including beads) of one plate into a 2 mL tube and add 960 μL (ratio 1/1) Bead Binding Buffer undetermined, Bead Binding Buffer Vortex to mix the sample and bead binding buffer.
00:05:00, binding of the cDNA onto the beads Undetermined, Room temperature
Place on magnet stand until clear
Discard supernatant
Wash twice with 2 mL 80% ethanol (while on magnet) and discard supernatant undetermined, 80% ethanol (freshly prepared)
00:05:00, air dry beads
Elute cDNA in 17 μL UltraPure Water & transfer to new tube undetermined, UltraPure Water Avoid transfering beads as these can inhibit the downstream PCR.
To the 17 μl cDNA, add: undetermined, Exonuclease I Buffer (10x) undetermined, Exonuclease I (20 U/μl)
In a thermocycler with heated lid, incubate: Undetermined, 20 min (ExoI digest) Undetermined, 10 min (Heat inactivation) Undetermined, ∞ (Store)
Prepare PreAmplification Mix as follows: %s %s
Add 30 µL of PreAmplification Mix directly to the Exonuclease I digested sample. undetermined, PreAmplification Mix

In a thermocycler with heated lead, incubate as follows: %s %s Cylce number highly depends on the input amount and should be optimized depending on the specific celltype used in the experiment. For ES cells, 13-15 cycles are sufficient.

Prepare Clean-up Beads: %s %s -Add all ingredients into a 50 mL falcon tube, but do not add the total amount of water until after PEG is completely solubilized -Incubate at 40°C and vortex regularly until PEG is completely dissolved- Resuspend bead stock carefully (Sera-Mag Speed Beads)- Pipette 1000 μL of bead suspension into a 1.5 mL tube- Place on magnet stand -Remove supernatant -Add 1000 μL 10 mM Tris-HCl, pH 8.0, 1 mM EDTA (TE), and resuspend beads -Place on magnet stand -Remove supernatant -Repeat wash one more time -Add 900 μL 10 mM Tris-HCl, pH 8.0, 1 mM EDTA (TE), and resuspend beads -Add to PEG solution above and mix well. Beads should be prepared ahead of time and can be stored at 4 °C or room temperature.
Mix PreAmplification PCR with 40 μL Clean-up Beads (1/0.8 ratio) undetermined, Clean-up Beads
00:05:00, binding of the cDNA onto the beads
Place on magnet until clear and discard supernatant
Wash twice with 150 µL 80% ethanol (while on magnet) and discard supernatant undetermined, 80% ethanol (freshly prepared)
00:05:00, air dry beads
Elute cDNA in 15 μL UltraPure Water & transfer to new tube undetermined, UltraPure Water

Quantify the cDNA using the Quant-iT PicoGreen dsDNA assay kit or equivalent Qubit following the manufacturer's protocol. Use 1 μl of clean cDNA for quantification. cDNA concentration should be > 1 ng/μl, depending on cell type and cycle number
Optional: Quality check the cDNA using the Agilent 2100 Bioanalyzer with High Sensitivity DNA Analysis Kits.
Prepare Tagmentation Mix and dispense 19 μL to a new 96-well plate. %s %s undetermined, Tagmentation Mix
Dilute cDNA to 0.8 ng/µL and add 1 µL to each reaction. undetermined, cDNA (0.8 ng/µL)
In a thermocycler with heated lid, incubate as follows: Undetermined, Tagmentation 00:10:00, Tagmentation
To stop the reaction, add 5 μL NT buffer to each reaction and mix by pipetting up and down. undetermined, NT Buffer 00:05:00, Incubation at room temperature
Prepare 3' Enrichment PCR Mix as follows and add 24.5 µL to each tagmentation reaction. %s %s undetermined, 3' Enrichment PCR Mix
Add 0.5 μL of i7 index primer (5 μM) undetermined, i7 index primer (5 μM)
In a thermocycler with heated lead, incubate as follows: %s %s
Mix Index PCR with 50 μL Clean-up Beads (1/1 ratio) undetermined, Clean-up Beads
00:05:00, binding of DNA onto the beads
Place on magnet until clear and discard supernatant
Wash twice with 150 µL 80% ethanol (while on magnet) and discard supernatant undetermined, 80% ethanol (freshly prepared)
00:05:00, air dry beads
Elute cDNA in 20 μL UltraPure Water & transfer to new tube undetermined, UltraPure Water
Load complete library onto an 2% Agarose E-Gel EX and run for 10 minutes. 00:10:00
As soon as the Gel run has finished open the Gel framing using the Gel opening tool
Excise the Library from 300bp to 900bp using a clean scalpel

Gel purify the slice using the Qiagen MinElute Kit following manufacturer's guidelines: Add 450 µL Buffer QG Dissolve the gel slice in QG for 10 min @ 42 °C Add 150 μL Isopropanol to the sample and mix by inverting Transfer sample to spin column and centrifuge at 16 000 x g for 1 min Discard flow through and add 500 μL Buffer QG Centrifuge at 16 000 x g for 1 min and discard flow through Add 700 μL Buffer PE Centrifuge at 16 000 x g for 1 min and discard flow through Centrifuge again at 16 000 x g for 1 min to remove residual ethanol Transfer column to a new 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube Add 20 μL H2O to column and incubate for 1 min Centrifuge at 16 000 x g for 1 min to elute and discard the spin column The Monarch DNA Gel Extraction Kit (NEB T1020L) can also be used.

Quantify and quality control the library using the Agilent 2100 Bioanalyzer with High Sensitivity DNA Analysis Kits. Load both the library and a 1/10 dilution on two different lanes of the chip. If cDNA was not quality controlled previously, it is strongly suggested to run cDNA on the same chip. Successful libraries will typically exceed 3-5 ng/μl concentration.
If pooling several libraries, combine equal molar amounts.

Sequence your library on any compatible Illumina sequencer. Dilute libraries to recommended molarity according to Illumina's recommendations (eg. 2 nM). Select the following paired-end read-length settings: %s %s Read Cycles Content Read 1 16 Cell barcode & UMI Index 1 8 i7 Index Index 2 0 Read 2 50 cDNA fragment
Download and install zUMIs including all dependencies. zUMIs Linux https://github.com/sdparekh/zUMIs
Copy the sequencing data from the sequencer and run bcl2fastq without demultiplexing. bcl2fastq --use-bases-mask Y16,I8,Y50 --create-fastq-for-index-reads

Run zUMIs by specifying your parameters in the associated yaml file or using the YAML config Rshiny application . Below is an example yaml file for a typical gmcSCRB-seq run. project: Example_project_namesequence_files: file1: name: /data/R2.fq.gz base_definition: BC(1-8) file2: name: /data/R1.fq.gz base_definition: - BC(1-6) - UMI(7-16) file3: name: /data/R3.fq.gz base_definition: cDNA(1-50)reference: STAR_index: /data/STAR5idx GTF_file: /data/Species.gtf additional_STAR_params: '' additional_files: /data/ERCC92.faout_dir: /data/zUMIsnum_threads: 15mem_limit: 0filter_cutoffs: BC_filter: num_bases: 1 phred: 20 UMI_filter: num_bases: 1 phred: 20barcodes: barcode_num: ~ barcode_file: /data/bc.txt automatic: no BarcodeBinning: 0 nReadsperCell: 100counting_opts: introns: yes downsampling: '0' strand: 0 Ham_Dist: 0 velocyto: no primaryHit: yes twoPass: yesmake_stats: yeswhich_Stage: FilteringRscript_exec: RscriptSTAR_exec: STARpigz_exec: pigzsamtools_exec: samtoolszUMIs_directory: /data/zUMIs2/zUMIsread_layout: SE Example zUMIs yaml
Seal the plate and vortex to mix the bead-lysate mixture. Briefly spin down the plate (<200 rcf, <10 sec) so all of the mixture is at the bottom of the well but the beads are still in solution.
00:05:00, air dry beads Depending on lab temperature and humidity, drying times can vary.
Wash twice with 50 uL 80% ethanol (while on magnet) and discard supernatant 50 µL, 80% ethanol (freshly prepared)
Discard supernatant
Place on magnet stand until clear
Incubate for 5 minutes at RT. 00:05:00, binding of the cDNA onto the beads Undetermined, Room temperature
Once drying is complete, add 4 μL H2O to each well. 4 µL, H2O
Add 1 μL of barcoded oligo-dT primer [2 μM] (E3V6NEXT adapter) to each well. 1 µL, barcoded oligo-dT primer [2 μM] (E3V6NEXT adapter)
Vortex to mix the bead-cDNA mixture. Briefly spin down the plate (<200 rcf, <10 sec) so all of the mixture is at the bottom of the well but the beads are still in solution.

Prepare Bead Binding Buffer %s %s -Add all ingredients into a 50 mL falcon tube, but do not add the total amount of water until after PEG is completely solubilized -Incubate at 40°C and vortex regularly until PEG is completely dissolved
